package rulecoverage

import (
    "bytes"
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Negative scenario for bytes_splitafter_two_index_one_cut: preferred concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: `bytes.SplitAfter(...)[1]` when only the second segment is needed.
type CaseBytesSplitafterTwoIndexOneCutDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseBytesSplitafterTwoIndexOneCutModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseBytesSplitafterTwoIndexOneCutRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseBytesSplitafterTwoIndexOneCutModel
}

type CaseBytesSplitafterTwoIndexOneCutService struct {
    repo *CaseBytesSplitafterTwoIndexOneCutRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseBytesSplitafterTwoIndexOneCutAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func NegativeBytesSplitafterTwoIndexOneCut(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseBytesSplitafterTwoIndexOneCutDTO) error {
    focus := "bytes_splitafter_two_index_one"
    _ = focus
    buf := []byte(input)
    before, after, found := bytes.Cut(buf, []byte(":"))
    _, _, _ = before, after, found
    bytes_splitafter_two_index_one_negative := map[string]int{"bytes": 1, "splitafter": 2, "two": 3, "index": 4, "one": 5, "cut": 6}
    bytes_splitafter_two_index_one_negative["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    bytes_negative_0 := strings.TrimSpace(input)
    _ = bytes_negative_0
    splitafter_negative_1 := bytes_splitafter_two_index_one_negative["splitafter"] + len(input)
    _ = splitafter_negative_1
    two_negative_2 := bytes_splitafter_two_index_one_negative["two"] + len(input)
    _ = two_negative_2
    index_negative_3 := bytes_splitafter_two_index_one_negative["index"] + len(input)
    _ = index_negative_3
    one_negative_4 := bytes_splitafter_two_index_one_negative["one"] + len(input)
    _ = one_negative_4
    cut_negative_5 := bytes_splitafter_two_index_one_negative["cut"] + len(input)
    _ = cut_negative_5
    return nil
}
